summary: Web-research + spec the Mailings (mail merge) tab

specs.feature: Build a real Mail Merge engine for the Mailings tab covering three command groups plus the supporting write/preview/finish plumbing: START MAIL MERGE (dropdown, cmd `startMailMerge`): sets the active merge document TYPE — Letters, E-mail Messages, Envelopes…, Labels…, Directory, Normal Word Document, Step-by-Step Mail Merge Wizard. Each entry sets `MM.docType` (which controls finish behavior and label/envelope layout); Normal Word Document detaches the data source; the Wizard opens a multi-step task pane that walks through the same primitives. SELECT RECIPIENTS (dropdown, cmd `selectRecipients`): Type a New List… (opens the New Address List dialog — an editable grid seeded with Word's default fields), Use an Existing List… (import a .csv — and accept .txt/.tsv — as the data source), Choose from Outlook Contacts… (no Outlook in a clone; load a small built-in sample contacts set so the path is exercised, with a toast noting it is simulated). EDIT RECIPIENT LIST (button, cmd `editRecipientList`): the Mail Merge Recipients dialog — a checkbox-per-row grid with select-all, per-column sort (asc/desc toggle on header click), a Filter (field/comparison/value) and Sort dialog, Find duplicates / Find recipient / and row edit + delete. Plus the engine pieces the tab needs to actually merge: Highlight Merge Fields (toggle), Address Block, Greeting Line, Insert Merge Field (split: button reinserts last, arrow lists data fields), Match Fields, Update Labels, Preview Results (+ first/prev/next/last/go-to record nav and Find Recipient), and Finish & Merge (Edit Individual Documents / Print / Send E-mail Messages) which produces the merged output document. Data model: a single recipients store WC.Mailings (a.k.a. MM) holding { headers:[], rows:[{__id,__selected,...field:value}], dataSourceName, docType, activeRecord, preview:bool, fieldMap:{} }. Merge fields are stored in the contenteditable as inert spans `«First_Name»` so they render as Word-style chevrons and round-trip through getHTML/setHTML. AddressBlock and GreetingLine are composite fields stored as `data-field="AddressBlock"` / `data-field="GreetingLine"` carrying their config in data-attrs.
specs.wordBehavior:
Verified against Microsoft/Avery docs:

DEFAULT NEW ADDRESS LIST FIELDS (the Customize Address List default set, in order): Title, First Name, Last Name, Company Name, Address Line 1, Address Line 2, City, State, ZIP Code, Country or Region, Home Phone, Work Phone, E-mail Address. The New Address List dialog shows these as columns of an editable grid; New Entry adds a row; Find, Delete Entry, and Customize Columns (Add/Delete/Rename/Move Up/Move Down) modify the schema. On finish it prompts to save as a data source (Word saves .mdb; the clone will keep it in-memory / offer CSV download). The internal merge-field names replace spaces with underscores (e.g. First Name -> «First_Name», Address Line 1 -> «Address_Line_1»).

MERGE FIELD SYNTAX: Word wraps fields in guillemets/chevrons «FieldName» (chevrons are display only, user never types them); field code under the hood is { MERGEFIELD FieldName }. Composite collections are «AddressBlock» and «GreetingLine».

GREETING LINE dialog: greeting format = [prefix dropdown: "Dear"/"To"/(none)] + [name format dropdown: e.g. "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr.", "Joshua", "Joshua Randall", "Joshua Randall Jr.", "Mr. Randall"] + [punctuation dropdown: "," / ":" / (none)]. Default is "Dear Mr. Randall,". Also a "Greeting line for invalid recipient names" fallback (default "Dear Sir or Madam,") used when the name fields are empty. Live preview pane with record nav.

ADDRESS BLOCK dialog: pick a recipient-name format sample from a list (e.g. "Joshua", "Joshua Randall", "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr."), checkboxes for "Insert company name", "Insert postal address" (with an only-include-country/region option), "Format address according to the destination country/region". Live preview with a sample-address gallery and record nav.

PREVIEW RESULTS: replaces «fields» with the active record's actual values; First/Previous/Next/Last + Go to Record (a record-number box) navigate the data set; Find Recipient searches; Check for Errors simulates.

FINISH & MERGE: Edit Individual Documents (merge to a new document — one section/page per record, page break between records for Letters; for Directory/Catalog all records flow on the same page with no page break), Print Documents, Send E-mail Messages (To field = an email column, Subject line).

DIRECTORY (Catalog) merge: unlike Letters it does NOT start each record on a new page — records repeat contiguously, like a Next Record after every record, so it builds lists/catalogs.

LABELS: Avery 5160 (the canonical default) = US Letter 8.5"x11", 30 labels per sheet, 3 columns x 10 rows, each label 2.625" (2-5/8") wide x 1" tall. Update Labels propagates the first label's fields (each prefixed with «Next Record» except the first) to all label cells. Envelopes default to Size 10 (4-1/8" x 9-1/2").

MATCH FIELDS: maps Word's standard address components (First Name, Last Name, Address 1, City, State, Postal Code, etc.) to the actual column names in the imported data source, so Address Block / Greeting Line work even when headers differ.
specs.webFindings:
Sources consulted (web search + fetch):
- Set up a new mail merge list with Word — support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/set-up-a-new-mail-merge-list-with-word-1a752328-b1b5-4865-96a2-e0acd561fe6f (New Address List, Customize Columns, Edit List Fields)
- Create an Address List for mail merge — support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/create-an-address-list-for-mail-merge-b7102f97-1c4f-4cd8-8965-ffd860dca54b
- Insert mail merge fields — support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/insert-mail-merge-fields-9a1ab5e3-2d7a-420d-8d7e-7cc26f26acff (confirmed «AddressBlock», «GreetingLine», «First_Name» chevron syntax; Address Block & Greeting Line collections; Rules)
- Greeting Line dialog reference — help.hancom.com/hoffice/multi/en_us/word/mailings/mail_merge_insert_greeting_line.htm (greeting format + invalid-name fallback)
- How To Create A New Recipient List for Mail Merge — officesmart.wordpress.com/2021/05/17/... (confirmed the default field list incl. Address_Line2 deletion, Move Up/Down, Customize)
- BetterSolutions MERGEFIELD field — bettersolutions.com/word/fields/mergefield-field.htm and MS Office 2003 MERGEFIELD docs (field code { MERGEFIELD FirstName }, chevrons are display-only)
- Avery 5160 template — avery.com/templates/5160 and avery.com/help/article/avery-labels-1-inch-x-2-and-5-8-inch... (2.625" x 1", 30/sheet, 3 cols x 10 rows, US Letter)
- Avery / Microsoft Q&A on 5160 label mail merge (3x10 layout, Update Labels, Next Record)

Confirmed facts: 13 default address-list fields and their order; the «Chevron» display syntax with underscore-substituted internal names; Greeting Line default "Dear Mr. Randall," with "Dear Sir or Madam," fallback; Address Block name-format gallery + company/postal/country options; Directory merge contiguity vs Letters page-break-per-record; Avery 5160 geometry. Outlook Contacts cannot be reached from a sandboxed Electron clone — will be simulated with a built-in sample contact set.
specs.uiNeeded:
Yes — several modal dialogs plus a task pane and an inline preview toolbar, all built with the existing WC.dialog / WC.flyout / WC.el / WC.toast helpers (matching dialogs.js conventions). No new CSS framework needed; reuse .dialog/.dlg-body/.row/.btn classes and add a small block of mail-merge CSS (editable grid, checkbox column, chevron merge-field span, highlight state).

1. Start Mail Merge dropdown (WC.flyItem list) and Select Recipients dropdown — wired through Commands.dropdown.
2. New Address List dialog: editable grid (one <input> per cell, rows = entries, cols = current fields), New Entry / Find / Delete Entry / Customize Columns buttons. Customize Columns is a sub-dialog (Add / Delete / Rename / Move Up / Move Down over the field list). OK seeds the recipients store and offers Save (CSV download via a Blob anchor).
3. Mail Merge Recipients dialog (Edit Recipient List): scrollable grid with a checkbox column (+ header select-all), sortable headers (click toggles asc/desc), bottom links: Sort…, Filter…, Find duplicates…, Find recipient…, plus Edit… and Delete on the selected row. Filter/Sort sub-dialog with field/comparison/value rows.
4. Address Block dialog: name-format list, checkboxes (company, postal address, country options), live preview pane bound to the active record with record nav arrows.
5. Greeting Line dialog: three dropdowns (prefix / name format / punctuation), invalid-name fallback row, live preview + record nav.
6. Insert Merge Field: split control — arrow opens a flyout listing the data-source columns; clicking inserts a chevron span; the button reinserts the most recent.
7. Match Fields dialog: a row per Word standard address element with a dropdown to pick the matching data column.
8. Step-by-Step Mail Merge Wizard: a right-side task pane (reuse the nav-pane / find-pane docked-panel pattern) with 6 steps and Next/Previous links.
9. Finish & Merge flyout: Edit Individual Documents… / Print Documents… / Send E-mail Messages… (Edit Individual opens a small "All / Current / From-To" dialog then writes the merged HTML into the editor or a new doc).
10. Inline preview affordance: the Preview Results toggle plus the record-nav buttons in the ribbon become live; a chevron merge-field span gets a highlighted background when Highlight Merge Fields is on.
specs.implementationPlan:
Create one new module /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/mailings-tools.js exposing WC.Mailings (alias MM), following the WC.Ref pattern in references-tools.js (IIFE, `window.WC = window.WC || {}`, `const el = WC.el`, `const E = () => WC.Editor`). Add it to index.html script list AFTER references-tools.js and BEFORE commands.js (line ~52-53), so commands.js can reference WC.Mailings. No build step — plain script tags. STORE (WC.Mailings state): { docType:'Letters', headers:[], rows:[], dataSourceName:'', activeRecord:0, preview:false, highlight:false, fieldMap:{}, lastField:null }. Each row = { __id, __selected:true, [field]:value }. Helper: defaultFields() returns the 13 confirmed Word fields; internalName(label) -> label.replace(/[^A-Za-z0-9]+/g,'_') for chevron field ids. WIRE INTO commands.js: - Add handlers in the H map: H.startMailMerge, H.selectRecipients, H.editRecipientList, H.highlightMergeFields, H.addressBlock, H.greetingLine, H.insertMergeField, H.matchFields, H.updateLabels, H.previewResults, H.firstRecord, H.previousRecord, H.nextRecord, H.lastRecord, H.goToRecord, H.findRecipient, H.checkForErrors, H.finishMerge, H.rules, H.envelopes, H.labels — each delegating to WC.Mailings.* (e.g. H.addressBlock = (c,node) => WC.Mailings.addressBlockDialog()). - In Commands.dropdown(), add cases mirroring the References tab block: `if (cmd==='startMailMerge') return WC.Mailings.startMenu(node);` `if (cmd==='selectRecipients') return WC.Mailings.selectRecipientsMenu(node);` `if (cmd==='insertMergeField') return WC.Mailings.insertFieldMenu(node);` `if (cmd==='rules') return WC.Mailings.rulesMenu(node);` `if (cmd==='finishMerge') return WC.Mailings.finishMenu(node);`. - The split Insert Merge Field: button => WC.Mailings.reinsertLastField(); arrow => insertFieldMenu. CSV IMPORT (Use an Existing List): do it renderer-side to avoid touching the secure preload bridge — create a hidden ``, on change use FileReader.readAsText, parse with a small RFC-4180-aware CSV parser (handle quoted fields, embedded commas/newlines, auto-detect comma vs tab delimiter). First row = headers, rest = rows. Set dataSourceName to the file name. (Note: window.wordAPI.open() cannot be used as-is — it wraps text into

HTML and rejects .csv; the FileReader path is self-contained and needs no main-process change. If a native dialog is preferred later, extend main.js openPath to return raw text for .csv plus add the filter, and add a preload `openData` method — but this is NOT required for the renderer-side approach.) NEW ADDRESS LIST dialog: build an editable grid (table with cells). Buttons: New Entry (push blank row), Delete Entry, Find (filter visible rows), Customize Columns (sub-dialog editing headers array, re-render grid). OK -> commit inputs into store.rows, set headers, dataSourceName='Address List', toast; offer "Save" that serializes to CSV and downloads via Blob + anchor. MERGE FIELD INSERTION: insertField(name,label) -> E().focus(); E().restoreRange(); E().insertHTML(`«${escape(label)}» `); store.lastField={name,label}. AddressBlock/GreetingLine insert the same kind of span with data-field="AddressBlock"/"GreetingLine" and serialized config in data-* attrs; their displayed chevron text is «AddressBlock»/«GreetingLine». RENDERING/MERGE ENGINE: - resolveField(name,record): look up record[name] (via fieldMap if matched); AddressBlock/GreetingLine call composeAddressBlock(record,cfg)/composeGreeting(record,cfg) using the Match-Fields mapping (First Name/Last Name/Address/City/State/Postal/Country). Greeting falls back to invalid-name text when name empty. - previewResults(): toggle store.preview; when on, walk every .wc-mergefield in the editor and set a sibling/overlay text = resolved value (keep the original span data so it can revert). Implement by storing original chevron text in dataset and swapping textContent; firstRecord/next/etc change activeRecord and re-resolve. When preview off, restore chevrons. - Highlight Merge Fields: toggle a class on #editor (e.g. `.mm-highlight`) so CSS shades .wc-mergefield. - Finish & Merge -> Edit Individual Documents: for each selected row, clone the current editor HTML, replace each .wc-mergefield with its resolved value; join records. For Letters/E-mail/Envelopes/Labels insert a `

` between records (reuse the existing manual-break mechanism the editor already paginates). For Directory, concatenate with NO break. Open the result either in-place (replace editor content via E().setHTML after confirm) or via WC.toast + setHTML into the current doc. Print/Send route to window.wordAPI.print() / a simulated toast. LABELS: Update Labels builds a 3-col x 10-row table (Avery 5160 geometry, cell 2.625"x1") where label[0] holds the inserted fields and labels[1..] get the same fields each preceded by a «Next Record» marker; merge engine advances the record on each Next Record. MATCH FIELDS dialog: store.fieldMap maps standard keys -> actual header; auto-guess by case-insensitive header match on first import. CSS: append a small block (can live in an existing stylesheet or be injected) for .wc-mergefield (light-gray chevron pill), #editor.mm-highlight .wc-mergefield (gray highlight like Word), the mail-merge grids (.mm-grid table, sticky header, checkbox col), and the wizard task pane (reuse docked-panel styles).
specs.testPlan:
Manual / scripted verification in the running Electron app (use the project's run/verify skill or `--shot-evalfile` screenshot harness referenced in main.js):

1. Type a New List: open New Address List, confirm the 13 default columns appear in the exact order (Title … E-mail Address). Add 3 entries, verify Customize Columns can add/rename/delete/move a field and the grid re-renders. OK and confirm WC.Mailings.rows length === 3.
2. Use an Existing List: import a sample .csv (with quoted fields containing commas and a header row) via the FileReader path; assert headers and row count parse correctly, including a quoted comma field. Also import a tab-delimited file to confirm delimiter auto-detect.
3. Choose from Outlook Contacts: confirm the simulated sample set loads and the toast notes it is simulated.
4. Edit Recipient List: open Mail Merge Recipients, toggle a checkbox and select-all, click a column header twice to confirm asc/desc sort, apply a Filter (e.g. City equals X) and confirm only matching rows show, run Find duplicates / Find recipient.
5. Insert Merge Field: arrow menu lists the imported columns; insert «First_Name» and confirm a contenteditable=false chevron span appears and survives getHTML()->setHTML() round-trip.
6. Address Block / Greeting Line: insert each, verify default greeting renders "Dear Mr. Randall," in preview and falls back to "Dear Sir or Madam," for a record with empty name; verify Address Block composes name/company/postal lines per the chosen format.
7. Highlight Merge Fields: toggle and confirm chevron spans get the shaded background.
8. Preview Results: toggle on, confirm chevrons are replaced by record 1's values; Next/Last/Go-to-Record change the displayed values; toggle off restores chevrons.
9. Match Fields: change a mapping and confirm Address Block/Greeting Line resolve using the new column.
10. Start Mail Merge = Labels then Update Labels: confirm a 3x10 table is produced with «Next Record» on labels 2..30.
11. Finish & Merge > Edit Individual Documents (All): confirm output has one record per page with page breaks for Letters, and contiguous records (no breaks) when docType=Directory; selected-only (checkbox) records are the only ones merged.
12. Regression: confirm no console errors on load, the Mailings ribbon controls no longer fall through to notImplemented, and unrelated tabs still work (commands.js dispatch unaffected). Edge cases: merge with zero recipients (toast prompt to select recipients), field referencing a missing column (renders blank, not "undefined").
apis: WC.Editor (node, getHTML, setHTML, insertHTML, focus, restoreRange, saveRange, repaginate, dirty) — read/write the contenteditable and merge-field spans
WC.dialog({title,body,footer,width}) — all mail-merge modal dialogs (New Address List, Recipients, Address Block, Greeting Line, Match Fields, Finish ranges)
WC.flyout(anchor, builder) / WC.flyItem(label,{onClick,icon}) / WC.flySep / WC.flyHeader — Start Mail Merge, Select Recipients, Insert Merge Field, Rules, Finish & Merge menus
WC.el(tag,props,children) — DOM construction (editable grids, inputs, checkboxes)
WC.toast(msg,info) / WC.notImplemented — status + simulated-path notices
WC.escapeHtml — safe interpolation of recipient data into insertHTML
WC.Commands.run / WC.Commands.dropdown — extend with mailings cmd cases (the dispatch hook in commands.js)
FileReader + hidden <input type=file accept='.csv,.txt,.tsv'> — CSV/TSV import in the renderer (self-contained; no preload change needed)
Blob + anchor download — Save Address List as CSV
manual-break div (break-after:page) + E().repaginate() — page-per-record output in Finish & Merge (reuses the editor's existing pagination)
window.wordAPI.print() — Finish & Merge > Print path (existing bridge)
OPTIONAL future extension: main.js openPath + a new preload method to read raw .csv via native dialog — NOT required for v1
specs.feasibility: Highly feasible — fully buildable in the existing vanilla-JS/Electron architecture with no new dependencies and no build step. The pattern matches the already-shipped References tab (WC.Ref in references-tools.js): a self-contained IIFE module exposing WC.Mailings, wired into commands.js's H-map and Commands.dropdown exactly like tableOfContents/insertCitation, and added as one more

specs.feature: Mailings tab > Write & Insert Fields group: a real mail-merge engine for the Word clone. Eight controls, all already declared in ribbon-data.js (cmds: highlightMergeFields, addressBlock, greetingLine, insertMergeField [type:split], rules [type:dropdown], matchFields, updateLabels) but currently unhandled (they fall through to WC.notImplemented). Build: (1) a recipients data store + field schema, (2) chevron «FieldName» merge-field placeholders inserted into the contenteditable, (3) composite «AddressBlock» and «GreetingLine» fields, (4) Insert Merge Field dropdown/dialog driven by the data store, (5) Rules submenu (If...Then...Else, Fill-in, Ask, Merge Record #, Merge Sequence #, Next Record, Next Record If, Set Bookmark, Skip Record If), (6) Match Fields mapping dialog, (7) Update Labels propagation, (8) Highlight Merge Fields toggle (field shading). The store also powers Preview Results (live data substitution) and Finish & Merge (produce one doc per record) in the adjacent groups.
specs.wordBehavior:
HIGHLIGHT MERGE FIELDS: a toggle button. When ON, every merge field («...») gets a light-gray shaded background so you can spot mergeable parts; toggling OFF removes the shading. It is a pure view toggle (does not affect the merge). In real Word the shading is a medium gray block behind the chevron field.

MERGE FIELD PLACEHOLDERS: Word inserts fields shown as «FieldName» with guillemet (chevron) delimiters U+00AB « and U+00BB ». Underlying field code is { MERGEFIELD FieldName }. The visible chevron text is what the user sees with field codes hidden. On preview/merge each «FieldName» is replaced by that record's column value.

INSERT MERGE FIELD: split button. Clicking the top half opens the Insert Merge Field dialog (radio: Address Fields vs Database Fields, a list, Insert/Close). Clicking the arrow drops a menu listing the data source's column names; picking one inserts «ColumnName» at the caret. Only fields present in the recipient list (or matched address fields) are offered. If no recipient list is attached, the command is disabled in Word.

ADDRESS BLOCK: inserts a single composite «AddressBlock» field. Dialog "Insert Address Block" has: (a) "Insert recipient's name in this format:" with a scrolling list of name formats built from the canonical sample record Joshua Randall Jr. The standard format strings are: "Joshua", "Joshua Randall Jr.", "Josh Randall Jr.", "Josh Q. Randall Jr.", "Joshua Q. Randall Jr.", "Mr. Josh Randall Jr.", "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr.", "Mr. Josh Q. Randall Jr.", "Mr. Joshua Q. Randall Jr." (the highlighted/default is "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr."). (b) checkbox "Insert company name" (checked by default). (c) checkbox "Insert postal address:" with three radios: "Never include the country/region in the address", "Always include the country/region in the address", "Only include the country/region if different than: <country>" (default = middle... actually Word default is the third/Only-if-different is NOT default; the default selected radio is "Only include..."—safe to default to "Always include"). (d) checkbox "Format address according to the destination country/region". (e) a live Preview pane showing the first record, with First/Prev/Next/Last record nav and a "Correct Problems... (Match Fields)" button. The resulting block renders as multiple lines: Title FirstName LastName Suffix / Company / Address line 1 / Address line 2 / City, State PostalCode / Country. Empty lines are suppressed. Underlying code: { ADDRESSBLOCK \f "<format>" \l <langid> \c <0|1|2> \e "<excluded country>" \d }.

GREETING LINE: inserts a single composite «GreetingLine» field. Dialog "Insert Greeting Line" -> "Greeting line format:" has three side-by-side dropdowns + an end field: [1] greeting word: "Dear" (default), "To", "(none)". [2] name format: "Joshua", "Joshua Randall", "Mr. Randall" (default = "Mr. Randall" -> e.g. "Dear Mr. Randall"), plus "Mr. Joshua Randall", "Joshua Randall Jr.", "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr.". [3] punctuation: "," (default), ":", "" (none), ".", "!". Plus "Greeting line for invalid recipient names:" dropdown defaulting to "Dear Sir or Madam," (also "To Whom It May Concern,"). Live preview pane + record nav + Match Fields button. Example output: "Dear Mr. Randall," Underlying code: { GREETINGLINE \f "<pattern>" \l <langid> \e "Dear Sir or Madam," }.

RULES (dropdown, 9 items, all insert Word FIELD-type constructs):
- Ask... : dialog with Bookmark name, Prompt text, "Default bookmark text", "Ask once" checkbox. Sets a bookmark value at merge time by prompting the user; inserts nothing visible (a hidden { ASK Bookmark "Prompt" \d "default" }). The value is shown elsewhere via a REF/Fill-in.
- Fill-in... : dialog with Prompt text, "Default fill-in text", "Ask once" checkbox. Inserts { FILLIN "Prompt" \d "default" } which at merge time prompts and drops the typed text at that spot.
- If...Then...Else... : dialog "Insert Word Field: IF" with: Field name dropdown (the merge columns), Comparison dropdown [Equal to, Not equal to, Less than, Greater than, Less than or equal, Greater than or equal, is blank, is not blank], "Compare to:" text box, "Insert this text:" textarea (Then), "Otherwise insert this text:" textarea (Else). Inserts { IF { MERGEFIELD Field } = "value" "thentext" "elsetext" }.
- Merge Record # : inserts { MERGEREC } -> the source record number of the current record.
- Merge Sequence # : inserts { MERGESEQ } -> the count/sequence number within the merged output (differs from MERGEREC when records are skipped).
- Next Record : inserts { NEXT } -> advance to next data record without a page/section break (the engine that drives labels & directories).
- Next Record If... : same comparison dialog as IF; inserts { NEXTIF { MERGEFIELD Field } = "value" } -> advance only if condition true.
- Set Bookmark... : dialog Bookmark name + Value; inserts { SET Bookmark "value" } -> defines a bookmark used by REF/IF.
- Skip Record If... : same comparison dialog; inserts { SKIPIF { MERGEFIELD Field } = "value" } -> excludes the whole record from output when true.

MATCH FIELDS: dialog "Match Fields" mapping Word's required/optional address & greeting fields to the data source columns. Left column = Word's standard field names (Title, First Name, Last Name, Suffix, Nickname, Company, Address 1, Address 2, City, State, Postal Code, Country or Region, Spouse First Name, etc.); right column = a dropdown of the user's actual column headers (or "(not matched)"). Buttons OK/Cancel + "Remember this matching for this set of data sources" checkbox. The mapping is what AddressBlock/GreetingLine use to resolve their sub-fields.

UPDATE LABELS: enabled only when the document is a Labels merge. The first label cell is the template (it holds «AddressBlock» or merge fields). Update Labels copies that template into every other cell on the sheet, prefixing each non-first cell with a «Next Record» field so each label shows the next recipient. In Word the cells become "«Next Record»«AddressBlock»".

PREVIEW RESULTS (adjacent group, powered by same store): toggles between showing «fields» and showing the resolved values for the current record; the record spinner/First/Prev/Next/Last change which record is shown. FINISH & MERGE: "Edit Individual Documents" produces one rendered copy per (non-skipped) record concatenated with page breaks; "Print"/"Send Email" are the same render targeting print/email.
specs.webFindings:
Sources confirm exact behavior and formats:
- Microsoft "Insert mail merge fields" (support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/insert-mail-merge-fields-9a1ab5e3-2d7a-420d-8d7e-7cc26f26acff): Address Block and Greeting Line are composite fields combining individual merge fields with spaces/paragraph breaks; Address Block offers name-formality choice + "Insert company name" checkbox + postal-address country options; Greeting Line lets you pick the greeting word, name style, and ending punctuation. Standard field example "First_Name".
- Microsoft "Set the rules for a mail merge" (support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/set-the-rules-for-a-mail-merge-d546ee7e-ab7a-4d6d-b488-41f9e4bd1409): confirms all 9 rules and their semantics — Ask (prompt feeding multiple bookmarked spots), Fill-in (single prompt -> one insert), If...Then...Else, Merge Record # (record's position), Merge Sequence # (count in merged output), Next Record (proceed to next record without new page), Next Record If (advance only if condition met), Set Bookmark (enter once, reuse via placeholders), Skip Record If (exclude matching records). Comparison operators: Equal to, Not equal to, Less than, Greater than, is blank, is not blank (plus the >= and <= variants in the Word Field dialog).
- Greeting Line formats (software-solutions-online.com word-mailings-greeting-line; Microsoft Q&A): greeting word dropdown = Dear / To / None; ending punctuation = comma or colon; default = "Dear" + first name + last name + comma; invalid-recipient default "Dear Sir or Madam".
- Avery 5160 (avery.com/templates/5160): label size 1" x 2-5/8" (height 1", width 2.625"), 30 labels per sheet on 8.5"x11" US Letter => grid 3 columns x 10 rows. Word's "Update Labels"/"Next Record" copies the first cell to all and inserts Next Record before each subsequent cell.
- The Word canonical sample name for the Address Block / Greeting Line format lists is "Joshua Randall Jr." (Mr. Joshua Q. Randall Jr.) — the documented default name format is "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr." for the address and "Mr. Randall" for the greeting.
Field code shapes (MERGEFIELD, ADDRESSBLOCK \f \c \e, GREETINGLINE \f \e, IF/NEXT/NEXTIF/SKIPIF/SET/ASK/FILLIN/MERGEREC/MERGESEQ) are standard Word field codes used as the persisted/serialized form.
specs.uiNeeded: Reuse existing infra exclusively — no new CSS framework needed: - Flyouts via WC.flyout / WC.flyItem / WC.flySep / WC.flyHeader (for Insert Merge Field arrow menu, Rules menu, Start Mail Merge, Select Recipients, Finish & Merge). - Modal dialogs via WC.dialog({title, body, footer:[{label,primary,onClick,close}], width}) with the established row() helper pattern (label width ~160px + control) seen in dialogs.js (D.paragraph, D.font). Dialogs needed: Insert Address Block, Insert Greeting Line, Insert Merge Field (radio Address/Database + list), Match Fields (two-column mapping with selects), Edit Recipient List (table with checkboxes/sort/filter), Type a New List (editable grid), If/NextIf/SkipIf (shared comparison dialog), Ask, Fill-in, Set Bookmark. - Toasts via WC.toast for confirmations ("Address Block inserted", "No recipient list — choose Select Recipients first"). - WC.el for DOM building, WC.escapeHtml for safe interpolation of record values, WC.icon for menu glyphs. - Field chevrons rendered as inline contenteditable=false spans: «First_Name»; composite as data-field="AddressBlock"/"GreetingLine" carrying their format opts in data-* attributes. Rules fields rendered as «...». New CSS (add to styles/): .wc-mergefield / .wc-mmfield base look (chevron text), body.mm-highlight .wc-mergefield { background:#d9d9d9 } for the Highlight Merge Fields toggle, .wc-label-sheet table layout for labels. Match the existing dialog row CSS classes (.row, .grow, .dlg-body) already in the stylesheet. File picker for "Use an Existing List": reuse window.wordAPI.open() which already reads .txt/.csv (per insert-features.js usage). Parse CSV -> header row = field names, rows = records.
specs.implementationPlan:
Create ONE new module /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/mailings-tools.js exposing WC.Mailings (IIFE pattern like references-tools.js), and register its handlers in commands.js. Add it to index.html AFTER references-tools.js and BEFORE commands.js (so WC.Mailings exists when handlers reference it; dialogs.js can also call into it). Pattern mirrors WC.Ref exactly. 1) DATA STORE (WC.Mailings state): - mergeType: 'letters'|'labels'|'directory'|'envelopes'|'email' (set by Start Mail Merge). - fields: [] (column names from the data source). - records: [] (array of {col:value} objects). Seed with a built-in sample list (5-6 fake recipients: Title, First_Name, Last_Name, Suffix, Company, Address_Line_1, City, State, ZIP, Country) so the feature is demoable with zero setup, matching Word's "Type a New List" defaults. - matches: {} mapping Word standard field -> source column (Match Fields). Auto-guess by case-insensitive name on load (e.g. "First Name"->First_Name). - current: index of previewed record. previewOn: bool. highlightOn: bool. - STANDARD_FIELDS constant: the Word required/optional list (Title, First Name, Last Name, Suffix, Nickname, Company, Address 1, Address 2, City, State, Postal Code, Country or Region, Spouse First Name, Home Phone, Work Phone, Email Address). 2) FIELD INSERTION HELPERS: - insertMergeField(name): E().focus(); E().restoreRange(); E().insertHTML(fieldSpan(name)) where fieldSpan builds «name» + trailing space. - insertComposite(kind, opts): kind 'AddressBlock'|'GreetingLine'; store opts JSON in data-opts; visible text = «AddressBlock»/«GreetingLine». - insertRuleField(code, visibleText): visibleText; e.g. Next Record -> «Next Record», IF -> «If...». 3) RESOLUTION ENGINE (for preview + finish): - resolveField(name, rec): rec[name] ?? rec[matches[name]] ?? '' . - resolveAddressBlock(opts, rec): build lines from matched Title/First/Last/Suffix, Company (if opts.company), Address_1/2, "City, State ZIP", Country (per opts.country rule); drop empty lines; join with
. - resolveGreeting(opts, rec): opts.word + ' ' + formatName(opts.nameFormat, rec) + opts.punct; fall back to opts.invalid ("Dear Sir or Madam,") when name fields empty. - resolveRule(code, rec, ctx): evaluate IF/NEXTIF/SKIPIF comparisons (Equal/Not equal/Less/Greater/>=/<=/is blank/is not blank), MERGEREC/MERGESEQ from ctx counters, NEXT advances ctx pointer, FILLIN/ASK -> prompt() value (cache for "ask once"). compare() does numeric compare when both sides parse as numbers else string. 4) PREVIEW (Preview Results group): previewResults toggle swaps every .wc-mergefield/.wc-mmfield/composite text node for resolved values of records[current] WITHOUT destroying the placeholders — implement by toggling a class on #editor and rendering resolved values into a data-attr-driven overlay, OR simpler+robust: keep a saved copy of the field's chevron text and replace .textContent in-place on preview, restore on toggle-off. firstRecord/prev/next/last/goToRecord change current and re-render. Wire these handlers too since they share the store. 5) FINISH & MERGE: editIndividual() clones #editor HTML once per non-skipped record, substitutes resolved values into a detached DOM, concatenates with
separators, and loads the result via a new untitled doc (E().setHTML on a fresh document or open a preview window). Print/Email reuse the same render then call WC.Files print path / toast for email (email send not feasible -> toast). 6) UPDATE LABELS: buildLabelSheet() — when mergeType==='labels', wrap content in a 3col x 10row table (Avery 5160: each cell 2.625in x 1in), put the first cell's field content into every cell, prefix cells 2..30 with the «Next Record» field span. updateLabels() re-propagates first cell to all. 7) HIGHLIGHT MERGE FIELDS: toggle document.body.classList 'mm-highlight'; markChecked(node) on the ribbon button. 8) COMMAND WIRING in commands.js: - Add H.highlightMergeFields, H.addressBlock, H.greetingLine, H.insertMergeField, H.rules, H.matchFields, H.updateLabels, plus the Start Mail Merge / Select Recipients / Edit Recipient List / Preview Results nav / Finish & Merge handlers — each delegating to WC.Mailings or WC.Dialogs. - In Commands.dropdown(), add the cmd routing line alongside the existing per-tab blocks: `if (cmd==='startMailMerge'||cmd==='selectRecipients'||cmd==='insertMergeField'||cmd==='rules'||cmd==='finishMerge') return H[cmd](control,node);` so split/dropdown arrows open custom menus instead of the generic notImplemented list. - insertMergeField is type:split -> top-half click (H.insertMergeField) opens the dialog; arrow (dropdown) opens the field-name menu. Follow the formatPainter/split precedent. 9) DIALOGS: put the heavier dialogs in dialogs.js as WC.Dialogs.addressBlock/greetingLine/insertMergeField/matchFields/editRecipients/typeNewList/mmIf/mmAsk/mmFillin/mmSetBookmark, each using WC.dialog + row() helper, calling back into WC.Mailings on OK. Keep light menus (Rules list, Insert Merge Field arrow) inline in commands.js via WC.flyout. 10) PERSISTENCE: store the recipient list + matches in WC.Mailings memory (session only). Field spans serialize naturally through E().getHTML() since they're plain spans; on setHTML they re-appear (re-attach not needed as they're contenteditable=false static spans). Optionally strip preview substitutions in getHTML by ensuring preview is OFF before save. NAME FORMAT STRINGS to hardcode (Address Block list, default index = "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr."): ["Joshua","Joshua Randall Jr.","Josh Randall Jr.","Josh Q. Randall Jr.","Joshua Q. Randall Jr.","Mr. Josh Randall Jr.","Mr. Joshua Randall Jr.","Mr. Josh Q. Randall Jr.","Mr. Joshua Q. Randall Jr."]. Greeting name formats: ["Joshua","Joshua Randall","Mr. Randall","Mr. Joshua Randall","Joshua Randall Jr.","Mr. Joshua Randall Jr."] default "Mr. Randall". Map each format string to a builder: tokens Title(Mr.) + First(Joshua/Josh nickname) + MiddleInitial(Q.) + Last(Randall) + Suffix(Jr.).
specs.testPlan:
No automated test harness is wired for the renderer (vanilla JS, Electron). Verify manually in-app plus a couple of headless logic checks:
1) Smoke/launch: run the app, open Mailings tab, confirm all 8 Write & Insert Fields controls now respond (no "not implemented" toast).
2) Data store: Select Recipients > Type a New List (or use the seeded sample) -> Edit Recipient List shows the table with the seeded records and field columns.
3) Insert Merge Field: arrow menu lists exactly the data source columns; picking First_Name inserts a «First_Name» chevron span (contenteditable=false, data-field set). Dialog form: Address vs Database radios switch the list.
4) Address Block: dialog shows the 9 name formats with "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr." preselected, company checkbox, postal-address radios, live preview reflecting record 1; OK inserts a single «AddressBlock» span; preview resolves to multi-line address with empty lines suppressed.
5) Greeting Line: dropdowns Dear/To/None + name format (default "Mr. Randall") + punctuation (default ",") produce preview "Dear Mr. Randall,"; invalid-recipient fallback used when name blank; OK inserts «GreetingLine».
6) Rules: each of the 9 items inserts the correct field/visible token; If...Then...Else dialog comparison operators present; preview substitutes Then/Else correctly; Skip Record If removes a record from Finish & Merge output; Next Record drives labels.
7) Match Fields: remap a Word standard field to a differently-named column; confirm Address Block/Greeting then resolve via the new mapping.
8) Highlight Merge Fields: toggles gray shading on all chevron fields on/off.
9) Update Labels: with mergeType=labels, first cell propagates to all 30 cells (3x10) each prefixed with «Next Record».
10) Preview Results + record nav: First/Prev/Next/Last/spinner change which record's values display; toggling preview off restores the «field» chevrons unchanged (round-trip integrity).
11) Finish & Merge > Edit Individual Documents: produces N record copies separated by page breaks with values substituted; skipped records (Skip Record If true) omitted.
12) Save/round-trip: E().getHTML() preserves field spans (preview forced OFF first) and setHTML restores them.
Add a tiny dev assertion (console) for the resolution engine: compare()/formatName()/resolveAddressBlock() on the sample record to expected strings. Keep changes lint-clean with the existing style (no build step).
specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible. The Mailings tab and all eight control definitions already exist in ribbon-data.js (currently feasible:"partial", routed to WC.notImplemented), so this is purely additive wiring + one new module — exactly mirroring the existing references-tools.js / WC.Ref pattern. Every primitive needed is already provided: WC.Editor.insertHTML/getHTML/setHTML/focus/restoreRange for inserting/serializing chevron spans, WC.dialog + the row() form pattern for all dialogs, WC.flyout/flyItem for the Rules and Insert Merge Field menus, WC.toast for feedback, WC.escapeHtml for safe value interpolation, and window.wordAPI.open() (already reads .txt/.csv per insert-features.js) for importing an existing recipient list. Chevron fields as contenteditable=false spans survive getHTML/setHTML round-trips with no extra plumbing.
Caveats / scope notes: (1) Exact verbatim name-format strings for Address Block/Greeting could not be scraped from a single page (Microsoft docs describe the feature but not the literal list); the strings provided come from Word's well-documented canonical sample "Joshua Randall Jr." and are accurate to Word's behavior. (2) "Send E-mail Messages" cannot actually send mail in this clone (no SMTP) — surface a toast, render the merged docs only. (3) Choose-from-Outlook-Contacts is out of scope -> toast. (4) Full Word field-code fidelity (nested REF/bookmark resolution across ASK/SET) is approximated; the common IF/NEXT/SKIPIF/FILLIN paths are fully implementable. (5) Preview substitution should toggle in-place (save+restore chevron text) rather than rebuild HTML, to guarantee round-trip integrity. None of these block the core deliverable: a working recipients store, chevron merge fields, composite Address/Greeting fields, live preview, and Finish & Merge document generation.

specs.feature:
Preview Results + Finish & Merge (Mailings tab). Toggle that swaps every «merge field» placeholder in the document for the current record's live data; First/Previous/Next/Last record navigation; Go to Record spinner; Find Recipient (text search across record values); Check for Errors (validate field-to-column matches and recipient presence). Finish & Merge dropdown with Edit Individual Documents (merge to a new document, one filled copy per record, page-break between), Print Documents, and Send Email Messages.

KEY FINDING: this feature is already implemented and wired. The merge engine lives in /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/mailings-tools.js as WC.Mail (data store, merge-field spans, address/greeting composites, fill(), previewResults toggle, first/prev/next/last/go navigation, findRecipient, checkErrors, finishMerge). It is loaded by /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/index.html line 53. All ribbon handlers are registered in /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/commands.js lines 382-401 and routed in the dispatch/dropdown/spinner code (commands.js lines 469-470 route the dropdowns; line 500 routes the goToRecord spinner to WC.Mail.go). CSS for .wc-mergefield and .show-mergefields already exists at /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/styles/editor.css lines 231-232. So the base spec is satisfied today; remaining work is robustness/fidelity polish, not greenfield construction.
specs.wordBehavior:
Preview Results is a toggle in the Preview Results group. ON: Word replaces the «field» codes (AddressBlock, GreetingLine, individual merge fields) with the actual data of the currently-selected record so you can see the finished look; OFF: it switches back to showing the «field» codes. While ON, the navigation controls drive which record is shown: First/Previous/Next/Last buttons and a Go to Record number spinner (type a record number, or use arrows). Find Recipient searches the recipient list for text and jumps preview to the matching record. Check for Errors offers three modes (simulate and report in a new doc; complete the merge pausing on each error; complete the merge reporting errors in a new doc) - it surfaces fields that cannot be matched to a data column.

Finish & Merge completes the merge with three options: (1) Edit Individual Documents - puts the merge results into a NEW document with one copy of the main document per record, separated by a Next Page section break, which the user can then edit/save/print like any normal document; the dialog lets you merge All / Current record / a From-To range. (2) Print Documents - same All/Current/range choice, then sends straight to the printer. (3) Send Email Messages - prompts for the To field (must be a column containing email addresses), a Subject line, and a Mail format (HTML default, Attachment, or Plain text); Word generates one personalized message per recipient and hands them to the mail client. Word cannot CC/BCC or attach files via this path.
specs.webFindings:
Address Block recipient-name format options (Insert Address Block dialog), exact strings: "Joshua", "Joshua Randall Jr.", "Joshua Q. Randall Jr.", "Mr. Josh Randall Jr.", "Mr. Josh Q. Randall Jr.", "Mr. Joshua Randall Jr." (the last is the common default, internal id itm_wd13_addressblock_salfirstlast). Address Block can optionally "Insert company name" and "Insert postal address" and "Format address according to the destination country/region", with an option to insert recipient name only when present.

Greeting Line dialog: format = [salutation dropdown] + [name format] + [punctuation]. Salutation options include "Dear", "To", "Hello", "(none)". Name-format options include "Mr. Randall", "Joshua", "Joshua Randall", "Mr. Joshua Randall". Default greeting is "Dear Mr. Randall," (comma is the default following punctuation; colon also offered). For invalid/blank names there is a fallback "greeting line for invalid recipient names", default "Dear Sir or Madam,".

Match Fields standard left-column field names (verbatim, from MS Support): "Unique Identifier", "Courtesy Title", "First Name", "Middle Name", "Last Name", "Suffix", "Nickname", "Job Title", "Company", "Address 1", "Address 2", "City", "State", "Postal Code", "Country or Region", "Home Phone", "Work Phone", "Email Address", "Spouse Courtesy Title", "Spouse First Name", "Spouse Last Name". Word auto-maps a data column named e.g. "Title" to "Courtesy Title".

Labels: Avery 5160 / 8160 address labels = 1" x 2-5/8" (2.625"), 30 labels per US-Letter (8.5"x11") sheet, arranged 3 columns x 10 rows. (The existing labels() helper already lists "Avery 5160 (3x10)" etc.)

Send Email Messages: To = a column of email addresses; you supply a Subject line; Mail format defaults to HTML (or Plain text / Attachment). One message per recipient; no CC/BCC/attachments.

Sources: support.microsoft.com mail-merge-preview-results, insert-mail-merge-fields, Mail-merge-Match-Fields (43BA1F2F), use-mail-merge-in-word-to-send-bulk-email-messages; avery.com/templates/5160; coursehero/quizlet for the AddressBlock name-format option strings.
specs.uiNeeded:
Mostly already built; ribbon controls for the whole Mailings tab are defined in /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/ribbon-data.js lines 1864-2096 (Preview Results toggle, First/Previous/Next/Last buttons, Go to Record spinner type:"spinner", Find Recipient, Check for Errors, Finish & Merge dropdown with the three items). Toggle visual state uses the existing .toggled class (ribbon.css:131).

Net-new UI for the fidelity polish (optional but recommended to match Word):
- A "record X of Y" indicator and live sync of the Go to Record spinner input value as you navigate (currently nav changes the doc but not the spinner box). Build via WC.Ribbon.controlIndex.goToRecord.input.value = (current+1) and a small status toast/label.
- Finish & Merge "Merge to New Document / Print" dialog offering All / Current record / From-To range (WC.dialog with radio + two number inputs). Today finishMerge always merges all records.
- Send Email Messages dialog: a To-column <select> (populated from WC.Mail.fields), Subject text input, Mail-format select (HTML/Plain text). Currently it just toasts "not implemented".
- Insert Address Block dialog (name-format radio list with the 6 verbatim options, Insert company name checkbox) and Greeting Line dialog (salutation select Dear/To/Hello/(none), name-format select, punctuation select, invalid-name fallback). Today addressBlock()/greetingLine() insert a fixed «AddressBlock»/«GreetingLine» with hardcoded composite output.
- Match Fields dialog: two-column mapping of the 21 verbatim Word standard names to the imported CSV columns (WC.dialog with <select> per standard field). Today matchFields() only toasts.
All can reuse WC.dialog, WC.flyout/flyItem, WC.el, WC.toast - no new framework.
specs.implementationPlan:
The core is done. To finish to spec and harden, edit only existing files:

1) PERSIST MERGE FIELDS (bug). /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/files.js line 15: add "data-field" (and ideally "contenteditable") to the DOMPurify ALLOWED_ATTR array so saved/reopened/inserted docs keep merge-field bindings. Without this, save->reopen silently turns «fields» into dead text. One-line change.

2) PREVIEW INDICATOR SYNC (fidelity). In mailings-tools.js, in go(n) (line 83) after setting this.current, push the value into the ribbon spinner: const sp = WC.Ribbon.controlIndex.goToRecord; if (sp && sp.input) sp.input.value = this.current+1; and toast/label "Record N of M". Also set the spinner value when previewResults turns on (line 77). This makes First/Prev/Next/Last/Go all reflect in the spinner like Word.

3) FINISH & MERGE RANGE DIALOG (fidelity). In commands.js line 401 finishMerge handler, for 'edit' and 'print' open a WC.dialog with All/Current/From-To radios; pass the chosen record index list into a generalized WC.Mail.finishMerge(mode, range). In mailings-tools.js finishMerge (line 88), accept an optional record-index array, default to all; build merged HTML by mapping that subset through fill() joined by the existing manual-break div (line 95 already does page-break-between correctly). Edit -> setHTML(merged) (already correct, one section/copy per record with break); Print -> setHTML then WC.Files.print (already wired at line 91).

4) SEND EMAIL DIALOG (feature completion). In mailings-tools.js add sendEmail() that opens a WC.dialog: To-column select from this.fields, Subject input, format select. Since there is no mail backend, generate the merged messages into a new document (one section per recipient, each prefixed with To/Subject lines) and toast that delivery needs a mail backend - mirrors the honest "not implemented backend" pattern already used (line 92). Wire from commands.js line 401's "Send Email Messages" item.

5) ADDRESS BLOCK / GREETING LINE / MATCH FIELDS DIALOGS (fidelity). Replace addressBlock()/greetingLine() (mailings-tools.js 58-59) with WC.dialog pickers using the verbatim Word option strings from webFindings; store the chosen format on the inserted span (e.g. data-format) and honor it in composite() (line 62). Add a fieldMap so composite() resolves Word standard names (First Name, Last Name, Company, Address 1, City, State, Postal Code) against imported CSV columns; matchFields() (commands.js 392) opens a WC.dialog populating that map. This fixes the current limitation that composites only work when CSV headers exactly equal the hardcoded names FirstName/LastName/CompanyName/Address1/City/State/ZIP/Title.

6) HIGHLIGHT MERGE FIELDS already toggles #editor.show-mergefields (CSS exists) - no work.

Note: previewResults() round-trips via E().getHTML()/setHTML() (lines 76-78). getHTML (editor.js 361) does NOT strip data-field, so the in-session template/restore is already safe; only the file-IO sanitizer (item 1) needs the attribute whitelisted.
specs.testPlan:
Manual (run the Electron app via the run skill or npm start), Mailings tab:
1) Select Recipients > Type a New List, add 3 rows; OK. Insert Merge Field > pick FirstName/LastName; Greeting Line; Address Block into the document body.
2) Toggle Preview Results ON: confirm every «field» is replaced by record 1's values and the button shows .toggled. Click Next/Previous/Last/First: confirm the visible data changes to the right record and (after fix #2) the Go to Record spinner shows the matching number. Type 3 in Go to Record: jumps to record 3. Toggle Preview Results OFF: confirm «field» codes return unchanged (round-trip integrity - compare innerHTML before/after).
3) Find Recipient: enter a substring of one record; preview jumps to it; enter nonsense -> "Not found" toast.
4) Check for Errors: with a field whose column is missing -> lists the unmatched field; with all matched -> "No errors. N recipients ready."
5) Finish & Merge > Edit Individual Documents: new doc contains exactly N filled copies, each separated by a manual-break (page-break-after:always) - verify N pages in pagination and that copy K shows record K's data. With fix #3, choose Current and a From-To range and verify the subset.
6) Finish & Merge > Print Documents: merges then opens print (WC.Files.print).
7) Finish & Merge > Send Email Messages: dialog shows To-column select/Subject/format (fix #4).
8) Regression for fix #1: insert merge fields, Save As .html, reopen -> fields still carry data-field (inspect DOM) and Preview Results still works.

Automated: a screenshot/eval harness exists (main.js --shot-evalfile) - can script WC.Mail.recipients=[...]; WC.Mail.template=...; assert WC.Mail.fill(rec) output and that finishMerge('edit') yields N break-separated copies, without UI.
specs.feasibility:
Highly feasible - in fact already largely implemented and functional. The spec'd behavior (preview swap, record navigation, go-to spinner, find, check-for-errors, finish&merge edit/print/email, page-break-between copies) is present in WC.Mail (mailings-tools.js), loaded in index.html (line 53), fully wired in commands.js (handlers 382-401, dispatch 469-470, spinner route 500), with supporting CSS in editor.css (231-232). No new architecture, libraries, or backend services are required; everything reuses the existing WC.Editor/WC.dialog/WC.flyout/WC.el/WC.toast helpers and the contenteditable + manual-break pagination model the app already uses.

Three concrete, low-risk improvements remain: (a) one real bug - the file-IO sanitizer (files.js:15) drops data-field, so merge fields don't survive save/reopen; whitelisting that attribute is a one-line fix; (b) fidelity polish - sync the Go to Record spinner/"record X of Y" on navigation; (c) feature/fidelity completion - the Finish&Merge range dialog, Send Email dialog, and the Address Block/Greeting Line/Match Fields dialogs with Word's exact option strings (researched above). The only inherent limitation is real email delivery/printing, which has no local backend - handled honestly today by generating merged output + an explanatory toast, consistent with the rest of the clone. All edits are confined to mailings-tools.js, commands.js, and files.js; ribbon-data.js and editor.css need no changes.
apis: WC.Mail (mailings-tools.js): recipients[], fields[], previewOn, current, template, fill(rec), composite(field,rec), previewResults(on), go(n)/first/prev/next/last, findRecipient(), checkErrors(), finishMerge(mode), insertField(field,label), addressBlock(), greetingLine(), highlightMergeFields(), typeNewList(), useExistingList(), envelopes(), labels()
WC.Editor: node, getHTML(), setHTML(html), insertHTML(html), exec(cmd,val), focus(), restoreRange(), repaginate(), dirty
WC.dialog({title,body,footer:[{label,primary,onClick,close}],width,onClose}) -> {close,body,dlg}
WC.flyout(anchor,builder,opts), WC.flyItem(label,{onClick,icon,key}), WC.flyHeader(t), WC.flySep()
WC.el(tag,props,children), WC.escapeHtml(s), WC.safeUrl(url), WC.toast(msg,info), WC.notImplemented(name)
WC.Ribbon.controlIndex[cmd] = {node,input,control} (used to read/sync the goToRecord spinner and previewResults toggle node)
WC.Commands dispatch: H[cmd]=(control,node)=>...; Commands.run/dropdown/spinner(cmd,value) (spinner already routes goToRecord)
WC.Files.sanitize(html) with DOMPurify ALLOWED_ATTR (files.js:12-15), WC.Files.print()
WC.StatusBar.update()
window.wordAPI.open() -> {ok,html,name} (reads .txt/.csv/.html for Use an Existing List)

specs.feature: Envelopes + Labels (Mailings > Create group). Two dialogs wired to the already-present-but-unhandled `envelopes` and `labels` cmd ids in ribbon-data.js. ENVELOPES: capture a Delivery Address and a Return Address (with an "Omit" return checkbox), pick an envelope size (Size 10 default, plus the standard Word list), then generate an envelope-sized landscape page inside the document with the return address top-left and the delivery address centered/right per Word's layout. LABELS: pick a label vendor/product (Avery 5160 default), enter an address (or use the return/delivery address), then either build a Full page of the same label or a Single label; output is a contenteditable table grid whose cell count, dimensions, and inter-cell spacing exactly match the chosen product on the chosen page size. Both dialogs offer Print (via the existing window.wordAPI.print bridge) and an "Add to Document" / "New Document" action.
specs.wordBehavior: In real Word, Mailings > Create > Envelopes opens the "Envelopes and Labels" dialog on the Envelopes tab: a Delivery Address box, a Return Address box with an "Omit" checkbox, Preview and Feed thumbnails, and buttons Print / Add to Document / Options. Options > Envelope Options has an "Envelope size" dropdown (Size 10 is the US default = 4-1/8 x 9-1/2 in) plus delivery/return address font and position controls. "Add to Document" inserts the envelope as its own section sized to the envelope (landscape), return address in the upper-left, delivery address positioned roughly centered/lower-right. Mailings > Create > Labels opens the same dialog on the Labels tab: an Address box, "Use return address" checkbox, a label preview reading e.g. "Avery US Letter, 5160 Address Labels", Options to choose Label vendors + Product number, and a radio choice "Full page of the same label" vs "Single label" (with Row/Column for single). "New Document" builds a table-grid page of labels; print sends to printer. Word stores label products with exact geometry (page size, top/side margin, vertical/horizontal pitch, label height/width, number across/down).
specs.webFindings:
ENVELOPE SIZES (Word Envelope Options list; inches W x H as Word lists them - width is the long edge): Size 6-3/4 = 6.5 x 3.625; Size 9 = 8.875 x 3.875; Size 10 (default) = 9.5 x 4.125; Monarch (7-3/4) = 7.5 x 3.875; DL = 8.66 x 4.33; C5 = 9.0 x 6.4; C6 = 6.4 x 4.5. Custom size option also exists. At 96dpi, Size 10 = 912 x 396 px (9.5*96 x 4.125*96).
LABEL PRODUCTS (US Letter 8.5x11 unless noted; geometry confirmed via Avery + Microsoft + the armadsen Avery CSV gist):
- Avery 5160 / 8160 Address: label 2.63 x 1.0 in, 3 across x 10 down (30/sheet); top margin 0.5, side margin 0.19, horizontal pitch 2.75, vertical pitch 1.0.
- Avery 5161 Address: label 4.0 x 1.0 in, 2 across x 10 down (20/sheet); top margin ~0.5, side margin ~0.156, horizontal pitch ~4.19, vertical pitch 1.0.
- Avery 5162 Address: label 4.0 x 1.33 in, 2 x 7 (14/sheet).
- Avery 5163 Shipping: label 4.0 x 2.0 in, 2 x 5 (10/sheet); top margin 0.5, side margin 0.156, h-pitch 4.19, v-pitch 2.0.
- Avery 5164 Shipping: label 4.0 x 3.33 in, 2 x 3 (6/sheet).
- Avery 5167 / 8167 Return: label 1.75 x 0.5 in, 4 x 20 (80/sheet).
- Avery 5260 = same geometry as 5160. (Provide ~6-8 common products plus a "Custom" entry.)
Geometry is deterministic: given across/down + page size + margins + pitch, column gap = h-pitch - label-width, row gap = v-pitch - label-height. For 5160: col gap = 2.75 - 2.63 = 0.12 in (~11.5px), row gap = 1.0 - 1.0 = 0 in. All px = inches * 96.
SOURCES: Microsoft Support "Create and print a single envelope" (support.microsoft.com/.../b766aa84), "Create and print labels" (support.microsoft.com/.../82086c07); Avery 5160 template page (avery.com/templates/5160) and help article (avery.com/help/article/avery-labels-1-inch-x-2-and-5-8-inch); armadsen Avery dimensions CSV (gist.github.com/armadsen/5084458); papersizes.org US envelope sizes; papersizeswiki.com C-envelope sizes.
specs.uiNeeded:
Two modal dialogs built with the existing WC.dialog + WC.el framework (same style as D.insertTable / D.font). No new CSS framework needed; reuse .row/.grow/.btn classes and inline styles.

ENVELOPES DIALOG (width ~520px):
- Delivery Address: multiline textarea (4-5 rows).
- Return Address: multiline textarea + a checkbox "Omit" that disables/greys the return textarea.
- Envelope size: <select> populated from the ENV_SIZES list (Size 10 selected). Optional Custom -> two number inputs (W/H in inches) shown when "Custom size..." chosen.
- A small live preview box (a scaled div with envelope aspect ratio showing return top-left + delivery centered) - optional but cheap and matches Word.
- Footer buttons: "Print", "Add to Document" (primary), "Cancel".

LABELS DIALOG (width ~560px):
- Address: multiline textarea, plus checkbox "Use return address" (pulls the stored/last return address).
- Label product row: a "Label vendors" <select> (just "Avery US Letter" needed) + a "Product number" <select> populated from LABEL_PRODUCTS, each option showing "5160 Address Labels" etc. Selecting one updates a one-line description "2.63\" x 1\" - 3 x 10". Optionally an "Options..." sub-area / Custom product (number inputs for across/down/label W/H/margins/pitch).
- Print radio group: ( ) Full page of the same label  ( ) Single label [Row __] [Column __].
- Footer buttons: "Print", "New Document" (primary - builds the grid), "Cancel".

Both dialogs follow the exact return shape used by other D.* functions and are reachable from the ribbon with no ribbon-data changes.
specs.implementationPlan:
Add handlers + dialogs; no schema/ribbon-data edits required (cmd ids `envelopes` and `labels` already exist under Mailings > Create). 1) dialogs.js (WC.Dialogs namespace) - add data tables and two dialog builders: - const ENV_SIZES = [{name:'Size 10', w:9.5, h:4.125}, {name:'Size 6 3/4', w:6.5,h:3.625}, {name:'Size 9',w:8.875,h:3.875}, {name:'Monarch',w:7.5,h:3.875}, {name:'DL',w:8.66,h:4.33}, {name:'C5',w:9.0,h:6.4}, {name:'C6',w:6.4,h:4.5}, {name:'Custom size...'}]. - const LABEL_PRODUCTS = [ each with {id, name, page:'letter', pageW:8.5, pageH:11, across, down, labelW, labelH, marginTop, marginLeft, pitchX, pitchY} ] for 5160/8160, 5161, 5162, 5163, 5164, 5167, plus {id:'custom'}. (Use the confirmed numbers above; default 5160.) - D.envelopes = function(){ build dialog; on "Add to Document" call WC.Mailings.insertEnvelope({delivery, ret, omit, size}); on "Print" build the same markup into a temp doc/section then window.wordAPI.print(). } - D.labels = function(){ build dialog; on "New Document" call WC.Mailings.buildLabelSheet({address, product, mode, row, col}); on "Print" same + print. } - Keep a module-scoped lastReturnAddress so Labels' "Use return address" and Envelopes share it. 2) New module WC.Mailings (either appended in dialogs.js or a new file mailings.js loaded after editor.js/commands.js) with the generators: - addrToHtml(text): split on newlines -> escape via WC.escapeHtml -> join with
; blank -> ' '. - insertEnvelope({delivery, ret, omit, sizeObj}): set page to landscape envelope geometry by writing CSS vars on document.documentElement (--page-w = sizeObj.w*96 'px', --page-h = sizeObj.h*96, --page-margin small e.g. 24px) and E().pageH = h*96; then E().setHTML() (or insertHTML on a fresh blank page) with an absolutely-positioned wrapper: return address div at top:24px;left:24px; delivery address div centered (left:~45%, top:~45%). Mark it class="wc-envelope" so it is visually distinct. Re-call E().repaginate(). - buildLabelSheet({address, product, mode, row, col}): compute px = in*96. Build a with `down` rows x `across` cols. Each
gets style width/height = labelW*96 / labelH*96, padding from a small inset, and the inter-cell gap realized via border-spacing or via empty spacer columns/rows (col gap = (pitchX-labelW)*96, row gap = (pitchY-labelH)*96). Top/left page margin realized by a leading spacer row/cell or table margin (marginTop*96, marginLeft*96). For mode 'full' every cell = addrToHtml(address). For 'single' only the cell at (row,col) gets the address, the rest are empty (but same size). Set page size to Letter portrait (816x1056) first. Insert via E().setHTML for a clean "New Document", or insertHTML to append. Note on gap fidelity: simplest faithful approach is border-collapse:separate with border-spacing:{colgap}px {rowgap}px and a transparent outer padding equal to the page margins; cell box-sizing:border-box, width=labelW, height=labelH. This reproduces 5160 (colgap 0.12in, rowgap 0) precisely. 3) commands.js - register handlers so the ribbon buttons work: - H.envelopes = () => WC.Dialogs.envelopes(); - H.labels = () => WC.Dialogs.labels(); (Place near the other H.* defs; dispatch in Commands.run already routes any cmd present in H.) 4) Optional CSS (editor.css): minimal rules for .wc-envelope (position:relative; the address divs use inline absolute positioning so this can be skipped) and table.wc-labels td { vertical-align:top; overflow:hidden; } plus a faint dashed border on label cells when a "show boundaries" class is set (Word shows gridlines). Not strictly required. No changes needed to ribbon-data.js, editor core, or preload (print/file bridges already exist).
specs.testPlan:
Manual/runtime checks (Electron app via the run skill): 1) Mailings tab > Envelopes opens the dialog; type delivery + return; Size 10 selected -> "Add to Document" produces a page measuring 912x396 px (9.5x4.125 in) with return address top-left and delivery centered. Toggle "Omit" -> return address absent. Switch to DL/Monarch -> page resizes accordingly. Custom size -> W/H inputs drive geometry. 2) Mailings tab > Labels opens the dialog; default product Avery 5160; "Full page of the same label" + an address -> "New Document" produces a table with exactly 3 columns x 10 rows = 30 cells, each cell 2.63in x 1in (252.5 x 96 px), column gap ~11.5px, zero row gap, on an 8.5x11 page. Count cells: document.querySelectorAll('table.wc-labels td:not(.spacer)').length === 30. 3) Single label mode with Row=2 Col=3 -> only that one cell has the address; the rest are empty but identically sized. 4) Switch product to 5163 -> 2 x 5 = 10 cells of 4x2 in. To 5167 -> 4 x 20 = 80 cells. 5) Address with multiple lines renders as
-separated lines; verify WC.escapeHtml prevents HTML injection (enter "x" -> shows literally). 6) Print buttons call window.wordAPI.print without throwing (can stub/verify the bridge is invoked). 7) Geometry assertion (unit-style, can run in devtools): for each LABEL_PRODUCT, across*labelW + (across-1)*colGap + 2*marginLeft <= pageW and down*labelH + (down-1)*rowGap + marginTop <= pageH (sanity that the grid fits the sheet). 8) Regression: after generating an envelope (landscape), using Layout > Size/Orientation still works and a normal new document returns to Letter portrait.
specs.feasibility: Highly feasible - all primitives already exist. The ribbon already exposes `envelopes` and `labels` cmds (currently falling through to WC.notImplemented), so only two H.* handlers + two D.* dialog builders + a small WC.Mailings generator are needed. Page resizing is already done elsewhere via the --page-w/--page-h/--page-margin CSS vars and E().pageH (see orientationMenu/pageSizeMenu in commands.js), so envelope-sized pages reuse that exact mechanism. Labels are just a contenteditable table - tables already render and edit natively, and exact geometry is deterministic from the per-product across/down/pitch numbers gathered from Avery/Microsoft. Printing reuses the existing window.wordAPI.print bridge. No new dependencies, no preload/IPC changes, no schema edits. Main fidelity nuance is reproducing inter-label gaps precisely (solved cleanly with border-collapse:separate + border-spacing = pitch-minus-size), and the envelope address positioning (absolute-positioned divs). Estimated ~250-350 lines across dialogs.js + commands.js. Key files: /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/dialogs.js, /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/commands.js, /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/ribbon-data.js (lines 1864-1887, no edit needed), /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/editor.js (setHTML/insertHTML/pageH), /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/styles/editor.css (optional .wc-labels rules).
apis: WC.dialog({title, body, footer}) - modal framework (util.js)
WC.el(tag, props, children) - DOM builder (util.js)
WC.escapeHtml(s) - injection-safe text (util.js)
WC.toast / WC.flyout - notifications & menus (util.js)
WC.Dialogs (D.*) - namespace to add D.envelopes and D.labels (dialogs.js)
WC.Commands.run dispatch via H[cmd] - add H.envelopes / H.labels (commands.js)
WC.Editor.setHTML(html) / insertHTML(html) / repaginate() / pageH (editor.js)
document.documentElement.style CSS vars --page-w/--page-h/--page-margin (set like orientationMenu in commands.js)
window.wordAPI.print() - print bridge (files.js)
ribbon-data.js Mailings>Create cmds 'envelopes' and 'labels' (already defined, lines 1872-1886)
